based_on.prompt: Build me an app that points me to the closest liquor store

recommendations.note: proper_noun_citation check: the ledger's proper_nouns[] is empty, which is correct. The literal prompt ('Build me an app that points me to the closest liquor store') names no trademarked product, app, format, hardware, or API — 'app' and 'liquor store' are generic nouns and 'liquor store' is correctly treated by ledger A4 as a retail category, not a named brand. No Researcher dispatch is owed.

discovery_high_importance_action check: both high-importance inflection points are resolved with concrete, evidence-backed action. IP1 was resolved via Researcher probe-IP1 — findings.json carries 6 static_canonical citations, each with a non-empty verbatim_excerpt, supporting the live OpenStreetMap Overpass API choice. IP2 was resolved via quick_reasoning grounded in canonical browser APIs (navigator.geolocation, Nominatim geocoding). IP3 is importance:medium, so the high-importance gate does not apply. No silent defaults on any high-importance point.

td_ip_source check: every machine-checkable assertion and inflection resolution carries a source field. The td_plan-sourced assertions (IP1.A4, IP2.A1-A3, IP3.A1, IP4.A1, and the S*.A* / DCA assertions) all describe properties of the app's own internal behavior (error handling, presence of UI controls, selection logic, vendored assets), not external-system claims — so self-referential verification is not a concern. The three assertions about the external Overpass system's properties (IP1.A1 endpoint URL, IP1.A2 shop=alcohol tag, IP1.A3 around filter) are correctly sourced as canonical_evidence with citations_pointer values into probe-IP1/findings.json.

first_contact_coverage check: all four first_contact_requirements are exercised. FC.1 (launches without error) -> S4.A1. FC.2 (location established, user not stuck) -> S1.A1/S1.A3/S1.A5 and IP2.A3. FC.3 (named store with address and distance) -> S2.A2, S3.A2, IP3.A2. FC.4 (actionable way to get there) -> S3.A3, IP4.A1, and the prompt_named_verb_assertion PNV.1.

telos_coherence check: telos is 'give the user a way to find the nearest store selling liquor relative to where they currently are.' Sections 1-4 form a direct chain to that end (location acquisition -> store data lookup -> closest-store selection/presentation -> app shell); section-5 is verification. No section is unrelated to the telos. The prompt verb 'points me to the closest liquor store' is exercised end-to-end by PNV.1 under production fidelity.
